name: writing-page-layout
description: Use this skill when you need to write code for a page layout in the Next.js
capabilities: Generates clean, modular page layouts for Next.js app directory
Enforces 40-line maximum per file for maintainable code
Separates TypeScript types and interfaces to dedicated files
Follows Next.js conventions for page structure
author: Emz1998
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. This skill contains only documentation (SKILL.md) with no executable code. Backticks flagged as 'shell command execution' are markdown inline code formatting. No cryptographic algorithms present. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Writing page layouts in Next.js can lead to inconsistent project structure and difficult-to-maintain code. This skill provides clear guidelines for creating modular, well-organized page layouts following Next.js best practices.
limitations: Only provides guidance for Next.js page layouts
Does not generate full application code
Limited to standard Next.js patterns and conventions
Cannot create complex nested layouts or advanced routing

useCases.targetUser: Developers new to Next.js or the project
useCases.title: Quick Start for New Next.js Pages
useCases.description: Get immediate guidance on creating well-structured page layouts when starting a new page in your Next.js project.

useCases.title: Enforce Team Code Standards
useCases.description: Ensure all team members create pages with consistent structure and proper separation of concerns.

useCases.title: Refactor Existing Pages
useCases.description: Use as a checklist to refactor and improve existing page layouts for better modularity and organization.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Page Layout
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the writing-page-layout skill to create a page layout for a new page in my Next.js app.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new page and need structure guidance

promptTemplates.title: Page with TypeScript Types
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the writing-page-layout skill to create a page layout that includes proper TypeScript type definitions.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a type-safe page with defined interfaces

promptTemplates.title: Optimized Page Layout
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the writing-page-layout skill to create an optimized page layout considering performance and maintainability.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a production-ready page with best practices

promptTemplates.title: Custom Convention Page
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the writing-page-layout skill to create a page layout following my project conventions at src/app/pages.
promptTemplates.scenario: Working with specific project folder structure

bestPractices: Keep page layouts under 40 lines for better readability and maintainability
Extract all types and interfaces to a separate types file in src/app/types
Follow Next.js app directory conventions for component organization
antiPatterns: Putting all types and interfaces directly in the page file
Creating page layouts exceeding 40 lines without clear modularization
Mixing business logic with presentation components in page files

faq.question: What does this skill actually do?
faq.answer: This skill provides instructions and guidelines for writing well-structured page layouts in Next.js. It does not generate code or execute commands.

faq.question: Do I need coding experience to use this skill?
faq.answer: Basic familiarity with Next.js and TypeScript is recommended to apply the guidelines effectively.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my files?
faq.answer: No. This skill only provides guidance through prompts. It does not read, write, or modify any files.

faq.question: Can I use this with plain JavaScript?
faq.answer: The guidelines focus on TypeScript patterns. For JavaScript projects, adapt the type separation guidance as needed.

faq.question: What is the 40-line limit recommendation?
faq.answer: This is a guideline to encourage modular design. Complex pages may need more lines but should remain well-organized.

faq.question: Does this work with the Pages Router?
faq.answer: The skill targets the App Router structure (page.tsx files). For Pages Router, similar principles apply but adapt to pages/index.js patterns.
